info.name: Soracom SIM Management API
info.description: Manage Soracom Air for Cellular SIMs and Subscribers including session control, plan changes, group binding, and lifecycle operations.

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target SIM. The SIM ID can be obtained from the [Sim:listSims API](#/Sim/listSims).

request.description: Changes status of specified SIM to "active".

response.name: The SIM's detailed information after the update.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: No such SIM found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: (DEPRECATED) Removes the credentials for Arc from a SIM

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target SIM.

request.description:
(DEPRECATED) Removes the credentials (public key) for Soracom Arc from the SIM.

**Warning**: Do not use this API.

response.name: The credentials for Arc have been removed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: (DEPRECATED) Attaches the credentials for Arc to a SIM

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target SIM.

request.description:
(DEPRECATED) Attaches the credentials (public key) for Soracom Arc to the SIM.

**Warning**: Please use the `renewArcSimCredentials` API instead.

response.name: Arc credential attach response

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Renew the credentials for Arc to a SIM

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target SIM.

request.description: Attaches Arc credential to a SIM and re-initialize a session at once.

response.name: Arc credential attach response

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Get data sent from a SIM to Harvest Data.

query.description: Start time for the data entries search range (UNIX time in milliseconds).
End time for the data entries search range (UNIX time in milliseconds).
Sort order of the data entries. Either descending (latest data entry first) or ascending (oldest data entry first).
Maximum number of data entries to retrieve (value range is 1 to 1000). The default is `10`.
The value of the `x-soracom-next-key` header from the previous response. Specify this to retrieve the next page.

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target SIM. The SIM ID can be obtained from the [Sim:listSims API](#/Sim/listSims).

request.description: Returns a list of data entries sent to Harvest Data from a SIM that match certain criteria. If the total number of data entries does not fit in one page, a URL for accessing the next page is returned in the `link` header of the response.

response.name: A list of data entries.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: Start time for the data entries search range (UNIX time in milliseconds).
End time for the data entries search range (UNIX time in milliseconds).
Sort order of the data entries. Either descending (latest data entry first) or ascending (oldest data entry first).
Maximum number of data entries to retrieve (value range is 1 to 1000). The default is `10`.
The value of the `x-soracom-next-key` header from the previous response. Specify this to retrieve the next page.

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target SIM. The SIM ID can be obtained from the [Sim:listSims API](#/Sim/listSims).

request.description: Changes status of specified SIM to "inactive".

response.name: The SIM's detailed information after the update.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: No such SIM found.

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target SIM. The SIM ID can be obtained from the [Sim:listSims API](#/Sim/listSims).

request.description:
Deletes and then reconnects the session for the specified SIM.

**Warning**: If you attempt to delete the session for an offline IoT SIM, the following error message will be returned:

```
Error: {"code":"SEM0016","message":"The specified subscriber is offline"}
```

response.name: The SIM's detailed information after the update.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: No such SIM found.

request.description: Enables termination protection for the specified IoT SIM (disables termination).

response.name: The SIM's detailed information after the update.

item.name: Send an HTTP request to a device using an IoT SIM

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target IoT SIM. The SIM ID can be obtained from the [Sim:listSims API](#/Sim/listSims).

request.description: Sends an HTTP request to a device using an IoT SIM and returns the HTTP response from the device in the API response body.

response.name: The HTTP request to the device was successful. The HTTP status code, headers, and body returned by the device can be checked in the response body.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: The specified IoT SIM does not exist.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name:
- The HTTP request to the device failed for one of the following reasons:
  - Could not establish a network connection with the device.
  - The device refused the HTTP connection.

- The HTTP response from the device was not received for one of the follow

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Sends ping requests to a SIM.

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target SIM. The SIM ID can be obtained from the [Sim:listSims API](#/Sim/listSims).

request.description: Sends ICMP ping requests to a SIM.

response.name: Result of the ping

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Send a command to a RTSP stream to a device using an IoT SIM

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target IoT SIM. The SIM ID can be obtained from the [Sim:listSims API](#/Sim/listSims).

request.description:
Sends a command to a device using an IoT SIM and returns a response based on specified command and options in request.

- An error occurs if the command does not complete within 10 seconds.

response.name: Command executed successfully. Response body will differ based on command and options in request.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: Invalid request.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: Failed to execute command due to authentication error. Please check the authentication information for Soracom API and RTSP server.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: The specified IoT SIM does not exist.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: Failed to execute command because the video stream received by Soracom API from the RTSP server is not H.264 encoded. Please verify that the video stream sent by your RTSP server is H.264 encoded.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Connect to a device using an IoT SIM via SSH and execute a specified command

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target IoT SIM. The SIM ID can be obtained from the [Sim:listSims API](#/Sim/listSims).

request.description: Connects to a device using an IoT SIM via SSH, executes a specified command, and returns the standard output and standard error output in the API response body.

response.name: The command was executed successfully. The command's exit code, standard output, and standard error output can be checked in the response body.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name:
- The command execution failed for one of the following reasons:
  - Could not establish a network connection with the device.
  - The device refused the SSH connection.

- The response from the device was not received for one of the following reasons:

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Connect to a device using an IoT SIM via TCP and send/receive data

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target IoT SIM. The SIM ID can be obtained from the [Sim:listSims API](#/Sim/listSims).

request.description:
Connects to a device using an IoT SIM via TCP, sends specified data, and returns data received from the device.

- The maximum size of sent and received data is 5 MiB each.
- If sending and receiving data is not completed within the time specified by `timeout`, an error will occur.

response.name: Data transmission and response reception were successful. Check the response body for the response from the device.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: Invalid request.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: Authentication failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: The specified IoT SIM does not exist.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: Data transmission and reception failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Send data using IoT SIM via UDP

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target IoT SIM. The SIM ID can be obtained from the [Sim:listSims API](#/Sim/listSims).

request.description:
Sends specified data to a device using an IoT SIM via UDP.

**Warning**: Unlike TCP, UDP communication lacks retransmission and sequencing mechanisms, which may lead to packet loss or out-of-order arrival.

response.name:
The data has been sent to the device.
However, due to the nature of UDP, packet delivery is not guaranteed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: Invalid request.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: Start time (UNIX time in milliseconds) of the period to retrieve the session event history.
End time (UNIX time in milliseconds) of the period to retrieve the session event history.
Maximum number of event histories to retrieve. However, the number of event histories returned may be less than the specified value.
The value of the `x-soracom-next-key` header from the previous response. Specify this to retrieve the next page.

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target SIM. The SIM ID can be obtained from the [Sim:listSims API](#/Sim/listSims).

request.description:
Retrieves event history of session creation/modification/disconnection for the past 32 days for the specified SIM. If the total number of events does not fit in one page, a URL for accessing the next page is returned in the `link` header of the response.

Check the current session using the [Sim:getSim API](#!/Sim/getSim). To check the current session with this API, check the value of `event` in the latest session history. Refer to [Subscriber Status](/en/docs/air/subscriber-status/) for the meaning of a session.

response.name: A list of session events.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: Start time (UNIX time in milliseconds) of the period to retrieve the session event history.
End time (UNIX time in milliseconds) of the period to retrieve the session event history.
Maximum number of event histories to retrieve. However, the number of event histories returned may be less than the specified value.
The value of the `x-soracom-next-key` header from the previous response. Specify this to retrieve the next page.

item.name: Adds a new secondary subscription to an IoT SIM

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target SIM.
(Required) ICCID of the target IoT SIM. For IoT SIMs that do not support eUICC, the ICCID and SIM ID are the same.

request.description: Add a new secondary subscription ([additional subscription](/en/docs/air/subscription-containers/#adding-a-subscription) or [virtual SIM/Subscriber](/en/docs/arc/configuration/#adding-a-virtual-sim-to-an-air-sim)) to the IoT SIM.

response.name: The updated SIM profile

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: You specified a subscription that cannot be added. For example, `planP1` cannot be added to an IoT SIM in plan-D.

response.name: No such IoT SIM with the specified SIM ID and ICCID found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Cancel the OTA to the subscription container for an additional subscription

variable.description: (Required) The SIM ID of the target SIM.
(Required) The ICCID of the target subscription container.
(Required) The IMSI of the target additional subscription.

request.description: Cancel the OTA of the additional subscription before the OTA starts. Note that once the IoT SIM goes online and the OTA begins, it cannot be canceled.

response.name: A schema listing subscription containers and containing a map of PLMN codes to subscription containers.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: The specified SIM ID, ICCID, or IMSI does not exist.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: List IoT SIM status operation history

query.description: Start time of the status operation history entry to be searched (UNIX time in milliseconds). If not specified, `from` is set to the oldest status operation history entry time of the target IoT SIM.
End time of the status operation history entry to be searched (UNIX time in milliseconds). If not specified, `to` is set to the current time.
The value of the `x-soracom-next-key` header from the previous response. Specify this to retrieve the next page.
Sort order of status operation history entry. Either descending (latest data entry first) or ascending (oldest data entry first).
Maximum number of items to retrieve in one request. The response may contain fewer items than the specified limit.

variable.description: (Required) SIM ID of the target SIM.

request.description: List IoT SIM status operation history. Status operation history entry records what status operations were performed for which IMSI and which subscriptions, along with the operation time. It includes speed class, group ID, IoT SIM status, etc. If the total number of status operation history entry does not fit in one page, a URL for accessing the next page is returned in the `link` header of the response.

response.name: A list of status operation history entry of the IoT SIM.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: Start time of the status operation history entry to be searched (UNIX time in milliseconds). If not specified, `from` is set to the oldest status operation history entry time of the target IoT SIM.
End time of the status operation history entry to be searched (UNIX time in milliseconds). If not specified, `to` is set to the current time.
The value of the `x-soracom-next-key` header from the previous response. Specify this to retrieve the next page.
Sort order of status operation history entry. Either descending (latest data entry first) or ascending (oldest data entry first).
Maximum number of items to retrieve in one request. The response may contain fewer items than the specified limit.

response.name: Bad request.

(Required) Tag name to be deleted. This will be part of a URL path, so it needs to be percent-encoded. In JavaScript, specify the name after it has been encoded using encodeURIComponent().

variable.description: (Required) Transfer token. The transfer token can be obtained from [Subscriber:issueSubscriberTransferToken](#!/Subscriber/issueSubscriberTransferToken).

response.name: Transfer token has been invalidated and the transfer has been cancelled.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: Transfer token does not exist.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Initiate the transfer of Ready status SIMs (Issue a transfer token)

request.description:
Initiates the transfer of Ready status SIMs. To use this API, you need an API key and token issued with the source operator. When this API is executed, a transfer token is issued and sent to the destination operator via email. For more information about SIM transfers, please refer to [Transferring Ready Status SIMs](/en/docs/air/transfer/#transferring-ready-status-sims).

- To cancel a transfer, cancel each transfer token individually. If there is a possibility of canceling SIMs one by one, specify only one IMSI. To cancel, use the [Subscriber:deleteSubscriberTransferToken API](#!/Subscriber/deleteSubscriberTransferToken).
- When this API is executed, the status of the SIMs will change to "Transferring".

**Warning**: Do not change the status of SIMs that are in the "Transferring" status. If the status is changed, the SIMs can no longer be transferred using this procedure. For the procedure to transfer SIMs whose status has been changed, refer to [Transferring Other Status SIMs](/en/docs/air/transfer/#transferring-other-status-sims).

**Info**: The information of the destination operator can be confirmed by logging in to the Soracom User Console with the destination operator.

response.name:
OK. The status of the SIMs specified in `transferImsi` will change to "Transferring".

**Warning**: Do not change the status of SIMs that are in the "Transferring" status. If the status is changed, the SIMs can no longer be transferred using the API.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Accept the transfer of Ready status SIMs (Verify the transfer token)

request.description: Verifies the transfer token for SIMs and accepts the transfer. To use this API, you need an API key and token issued with the destination operator.

response.name: SIM transfer successfully accepted and processed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: Tag name for filtering the search (exact match).
Tag search string for filtering the search. Required when `tag_name` has been specified.
Tag match mode.
Status for filtering the search. Can specify multiple values delimited by `|`. Valid values include: `active`, `inactive`, `ready`, `instock`, `shipped`, `suspended`, and `terminated`.

Speed class for filtering the search. Can specify multiple values delimited by `|`. Valid values include:

- `s1.minimum`
- `s1.slow`
- `s1.standard`
- `s1.fast`
- `s1.4xfast`
- `s1.8xfast`
- `u1.standard`
- `u1.slow`
- `t1.standard`
- `arc.standard`
Serial number for filtering the search. Can specify multiple values delimited by `|`. Returns subscribers with serial number starting with the specified value(s).
Maximum number of subscribers to retrieve (value range is 1 to 100). However, the number of subscribers returned may be less than the specified value.
The IMSI of the last subscriber retrieved on the previous page. By specifying this parameter, you can continue to retrieve the list from the next subscriber onward.

item.name: Get location information for a cell tower

query.description: (Required) MCC - Mobile Country Code.
(Required) MNC - Mobile Network Code.
LAC - Location Area Code (for 3G).
CID - Cell ID (for 3G).
TAC - Tracking Area Code (for 4G).
ECID - Enhanced Cell ID (for 4G) - specify either `ecid` or `eci`. The result is the same regardless of which value is specified.
ECID - Enhanced Cell ID (for 4G) - specify either `ecid` or `eci`. The result is the same regardless of which value is specified.

request.description: Retrieves location information (latitude/longitude) for a cell tower based on cell information such as Cell ID. - For 3G, please specify MCC, MNC, LAC, and CID. It is possible to retrieve approximate location information without CID, but the accuracy will be low. 3G example ``` $ curl -X GET "https://api.soracom.io/v1/cell_locations?mcc=440&mnc=10&lac=196" \ -H "X-Soracom-API-Key: $X_SORACOM_API_KEY" \ -H "X-Soracom-Token: $X_SORACOM_TOKEN" ``` - For LTE (4G), please specify MCC, MNC, TAC, and ECID. 4G example ``` $ curl -X GET "https://api.soracom.io/v1/cell_locations?mcc=440&mnc=10&tac=5680&ecid=48872466" \ -H "X-Soracom-API-Key: $X_SORACOM_API_KEY" \ -H "X-Soracom-Token: $X_SORACOM_TOKEN" ``` The conversion of cell tower information to location information uses the OpenCelliD Project database. The specified cell tower information is used to extract the corresponding location information from the OpenCelliD Project database, but it is possible that the location information may not exist or may be different from the actual location. [![Creative Commons License](/assets/images/ccbysa_4.0_80x15.png)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/ "no-icon-external-link") OpenCelliD Project is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License

response.name: Location information for the cell tower.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) MCC - Mobile Country Code.
(Required) MNC - Mobile Network Code.
LAC - Location Area Code (for 3G).
CID - Cell ID (for 3G).
TAC - Tracking Area Code (for 4G).
ECID - Enhanced Cell ID (for 4G) - specify either `ecid` or `eci`. The result is the same regardless of which value is specified.
ECID - Enhanced Cell ID (for 4G) - specify either `ecid` or `eci`. The result is the same regardless of which value is specified.

response.name: Location information is not found for the cell tower.

item.name: List location information for multiple cell towers.

request.description: Retrieves a list of location information (latitude/longitude) for multiple cell towers based on cell information such as Cell ID. For 3G, please specify MCC, MNC, LAC, and CID. It is possible to retrieve approximate location information without CID, but the accuracy will be low. For LTE (4G), please specify MCC, MNC, TAC, and ECID. The conversion of cell tower information to location information uses the OpenCelliD Project database, so it is possible that the information may not exist or may be incorrect. [![Creative Commons License](/assets/images/ccbysa_4.0_80x15.png)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/ "no-icon-external-link") OpenCelliD Project is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License

response.name: List of location information for the specified cell towers.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
